result:
Have everything needed. Summary:

## 1. Lock field in `struct rq`
**File:** `kernel/sched/sched.h`
- `struct rq` definition: line 962
- Lock field: **`raw_spinlock_t __lock;`** — line 964 (comment right above it: `/* runqueue lock: */`)

Note the leading double-underscore — this isn't a plain `spinlock`/`raw_spinlock_t lock` accessed directly like in older kernels. It's a "hidden" field that must be accessed through accessor functions, because v6.6 has **core scheduling** (`CONFIG_SCHED_CORE`): when core scheduling is enabled, SMT sibling rqs share one lock (`rq->core->__lock`), so callers can't just take `&rq->__lock` directly.

## 2. Accessor / take-release helpers (not plain `spin_lock`)

**Lock pointer resolvers** (`kernel/sched/sched.h`):
- `rq_lockp()` — line 1235 (CORE) / line 1333 (!CORE) — returns `&rq->core->__lock` if core sched enabled, else `&rq->__lock`
- `__rq_lockp()` — line 1243 (CORE) / line 1338 (!CORE) — same logic using `rq->core_enabled`

**Actual lock/unlock primitives** (defined in `kernel/sched/core.c`, declared `extern` in `sched.h:1366-1368`):
- `void raw_spin_rq_lock_nested(struct rq *rq, int subclass)` — `kernel/sched/core.c:551` — loops re-reading `__rq_lockp(rq)` in case the core-lock pointer changes mid-acquire (core sched can be toggled), retries if it changed after taking the lock
- `bool raw_spin_rq_trylock(struct rq *rq)` — `kernel/sched/core.c:576` — same re-check pattern for trylock
- `void raw_spin_rq_unlock(struct rq *rq)` — `kernel/sched/core.c:600` — `raw_spin_unlock(rq_lockp(rq))`

**Inline wrappers** (`kernel/sched/sched.h`):
- `raw_spin_rq_lock(rq)` — line 1370, calls `raw_spin_rq_lock_nested(rq, 0)`
- `raw_spin_rq_lock_irq(rq)` — line 1375
- `raw_spin_rq_unlock_irq(rq)` — line 1381
- `_raw_spin_rq_lock_irqsave(rq)` / macro `raw_spin_rq_lock_irqsave(rq, flags)` — lines 1387 / 1401
- `raw_spin_rq_unlock_irqrestore(rq, flags)` — line 1395

Higher-level task-facing wrappers built on top of these: `rq_lock()`/`rq_unlock()` (with `struct rq_flags`, also doing `rq_pin_lock`), `__task_rq_lock()`/`task_rq_lock()`, `double_rq_lock()` — all ultimately call `raw_spin_rq_lock*`/`raw_spin_rq_unlock*`, never `raw_spin_lock(&rq->__lock)` directly outside `raw_spin_rq_lock_nested()` itself.

## 3. Two real call sites in `kernel/sched/core.c`
- `kernel/sched/core.c:616` — `raw_spin_rq_lock(rq1);` inside `double_rq_lock()` (line 609)
- `kernel/sched/core.c:636` — `raw_spin_rq_lock(rq);` inside `__task_rq_lock()` (line 627)

(Additional examples spotted for reference, not required: `core.c:794` `rq_lock(rq, &rf)` in `hrtick()`, `core.c:1070` `raw_spin_rq_lock_irqsave(rq, flags)`.)
